post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 242 (Newbie Game 95)

Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Read all the rules!


Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm your participation in the game by making a post stating the important rules
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (fdas) am the moderator of Mafia Game 242
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons


Role PMs
Quote:
You are a Vanilla Townie. You have no other power other than your vote.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a Cop. Every night, you may target one person and determine their alignment.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a Doctor. Every night you may heal someone, allowing them to survive if they would have otherwise died. You may not heal yourself two nights in a row.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a Tracker. Every night you may target someone and learn who they visited.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a Jailkeeper. Each night you may choose someone to put in jail. That person will be unable to perform any actions that night and night kills attempting to target that person will be blocked.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie. On the first time that you are attacked by the Mafia, that kill will be negated.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a Mafia Goon. During the night you may talk with your partner () here to discuss who you want to kill.

You win when all members of the Town are dead.
Quote:
You are a Mafia Roleblocker. During the night you may talk with your partner () here to discuss who you want to kill. You may also target someone and that person will be unable to perform any actions that night. Your ability takes precedence over the jailkeeper.

You win when all members of the Town are dead.



Alive (3)
++sheepsaysmeep
++atomicpianowitch
Lilathebee ChrisWren


Dead(6)
Awesomeness_in_a_bun (Mafia Goon)
mosspelt495 (Vanilla Townie)
Sky-Yuze (Vanilla Townie)
+Scipio1 (Vanilla Townie)
llr (Tracker)
soggydollar (Vanilla Townie)


Events
Day 1: Awesomeness_in_a_bun hanged
Night 2: mosspelt495 killed
Day 2: Sky_Yuze hanged
Night 2: No one killed
Day 3: Scipio1 hanged
Night 3: llr killed
Day 4: No Hang
Night 4: soggydollar dies


PM Prods
 


Warnings
soggydollar: editing


Do not post until this line is crossed out

The small town of AoPS-242 was founded by a group of people wanting to start a new life. Initially, things seemed to be going well. The town was successful and the people began settling into their new life. However, the peaceful life of the town was soon broken. The mayor of the town, fdas, was killed by a group of unidentified armed men. In order to find out the culprits behind this attack, the prominent members of the town came together to resolve this crisis.

Day 1 begins. Current time: 23:00:00 EDT 14 September 2021.

post_rendered:
Also
aroodman wrote:
Hello everyone,

It occurred to me that this never actually gets explained, so let me give you the really, really basic rundown of how this game works.

Everyone here is either town, who wants to kill the mafia by voting them out, or mafia, who want to live long enough to kill the entire town. Townies don’t want to get voted out, because that’s more time that the mafia isn’t getting voted out, and the mafia doesn’t want to get voted out for obvious reasons. Town has the majority of votes, so the voting will be, for the most part, meant to kill the mafia.

This means that everyone wants others to think that they are town.

This means you shouldn’t take it too seriously when someone says “I’m town” or “I’m scum” (scum meaning mafia). If they say “I’m town”, well duh, anyone would say that, and if they say “I’m scum”, they’re clearly joking.

Now, for voting people out, or hanging.

Often, on the first day, newbies will say things like “We shouldn’t hang today because we have no information”. The problem is that if we don’t hang, the next day will be the exact same but with one town player killed by the mafia. Night kills are guaranteed to hit town. Hangs aren’t. That’s why we need to hang as many times as possible.

Lastly, what will we actually do for most of this game? The bread and butter of Mafia is the day phases, where we just... talk. The core idea is that you can often figure out if players are town or mafia just by reading their posts. This is also known as scumhunting. Scumhunting is how the town wins the game, and pretending to scumhunt as mafia is how you make yourself look like a townie, so it’s how you make yourself harder for the town to find.

How exactly do you scumhunt? It’s often hard to tell. Plenty of experienced players struggle in knowing what to look for. There are some common tells that people use, like being suspicious of people who try to blend in, or being suspicious of people who overreact, or not being suspicious of people trying hard to scumhunt.

Unfortunately it’s never that simple. Most scumhunting is just intuition. Any of the above things can be benign in some contexts, good looks in some, and bad looks in others. It’s all on a case by case basis. Why do you think this person wrote this post? If the explanations for a townie writing a post are better than the explanations for scum writing the same post, it might earn a townread. If vice versa, maybe a scumread. If you can’t tell, maybe the one post just doesn’t matter. There are a lot of those posts that just don’t matter.

If you have an idea about whether someone is town or scum, don’t be shy, just say it. I and the other experienced players may appear to shoot your ideas down, but don’t be discouraged when that happens. We (me, Fvr_Vain, RTG) are here to help you learn the game, and trial and error is how you get there.

The word “scumhunting” might make you think you’re only looking for scum, but looking for town is also helpful; if you find a townie, that’s one less person you can accuse falsely. Finding town helps find scum this way. The same applies in reverse; if you find scum, you can name people that they aren’t likely to be partnered with, hence finding more townies. If you do both at once, you do both better.

Before we go further, do not claim your role for no reason. If you are a power role (any role with some special ability), you want to stay hidden so that the mafia doesn't just shoot you on the first night. If you aren't, you will narrow the possibilities of who can be one by claiming, which will make it harder for power roles to hide. For now, only claim if you are about to be hanged and the deadline is close.

post_canonical:
Also

[quote=aroodman]Hello everyone,

It occurred to me that this never actually gets explained, so let me give you the really, [i]really[/i] basic rundown of how this game works.

Everyone here is either town, who wants to kill the mafia by voting them out, or mafia, who want to live long enough to kill the entire town. Townies don’t want to get voted out, because that’s more time that the mafia isn’t getting voted out, and the mafia doesn’t want to get voted out for obvious reasons. Town has the majority of votes, so the voting will be, for the most part, meant to kill the mafia.

This means that [b]everyone wants others to think that they are town.[/b]

This means you shouldn’t take it too seriously when someone says “I’m town” or “I’m scum” (scum meaning mafia). If they say “I’m town”, well duh, anyone would say that, and if they say “I’m scum”, they’re clearly joking.

Now, for voting people out, or hanging.

Often, on the first day, newbies will say things like “We shouldn’t hang today because we have no information”. The problem is that if we don’t hang, the next day will be the exact same but with one town player killed by the mafia. Night kills are guaranteed to hit town. Hangs aren’t. That’s why [b]we need to hang as many times as possible.[/b]

Lastly, what will we actually do for most of this game? The bread and butter of Mafia is the day phases, where we just... talk. [b]The core idea is that you can often figure out if players are town or mafia just by reading their posts. This is also known as scumhunting.[/b] Scumhunting is how the town wins the game, and pretending to scumhunt as mafia is how you make yourself look like a townie, so it’s how you make yourself harder for the town to find.

How exactly do you scumhunt? It’s often hard to tell. Plenty of experienced players struggle in knowing what to look for. There are some common tells that people use, like being suspicious of people who try to blend in, or being suspicious of people who overreact, or not being suspicious of people trying hard to scumhunt.

Unfortunately it’s never that simple. [b]Most scumhunting is just intuition.[/b] Any of the above things can be benign in some contexts, good looks in some, and bad looks in others. It’s all on a case by case basis. Why do you think this person wrote this post? If the explanations for a townie writing a post are better than the explanations for scum writing the same post, it might earn a townread. If vice versa, maybe a scumread. If you can’t tell, maybe the one post just doesn’t matter. There are a lot of those posts that just don’t matter.

If you have an idea about whether someone is town or scum, don’t be shy, just say it. I and the other experienced players may appear to shoot your ideas down, but don’t be discouraged when that happens. [b]We (me, Fvr_Vain, RTG) are here to help you learn the game, and trial and error is how you get there.[/b]

The word “scumhunting” might make you think you’re only looking for scum, but looking for town is also helpful; if you find a townie, that’s one less person you can accuse falsely. Finding town helps find scum this way. The same applies in reverse; if you find scum, you can name people that they aren’t likely to be partnered with, hence finding more townies. If you do both at once, you do both better.

Before we go further, [b]do not claim your role for no reason.[/b] If you are a power role (any role with some special ability), you want to stay hidden so that the mafia doesn't just shoot you on the first night. If you aren't, you will narrow the possibilities of who can be one by claiming, which will make it harder for power roles to hide. For now, only claim if you are about to be hanged and the deadline is close.

Oh btw I’m town. Oh btw I’m scum.

Let the games begin.[/quote]

post_rendered:
Ok, here for a while.

These 3 week days are really interesting. I’m used to faster paced days so this will definitely be an adjustment. There isn’t a rush but 3 week days are definitely more scum-sided. So take that how you will, if you want to win as town, it’s better to post a lot (even with long days) because it adds to discussion obviously but it also increases the social dynamics and makes us harder for scum to individually manipulate.

Anyways I’m atomicpianowitch, I think it’s a good idea to do not really an RQS but like nickname and pronouns and stuff. It’s fine if you call me apw or piano, I use he/him pronouns, they/them is also fine if that’s something you tend use generically on the internet.

Some quick things to know about me for meta purposes (because no one here has ever played with me besides sheep)
I post a lot, and my posts tend to be long. I will ramble a lot, and not really get to the point, because that’s how I tend to talk when I am really passionate or interested in something. The exception to long walls of text is when there is someone else actively in the thread. I always try to engage in real-time conversation in the thread, because it makes it much harder for scum to hide and adds a different perspective to most reads. Mainly, I rely on the strength of the crowd, and working as a team player, rather than using my own personal reads to find scum. My individual reads are not usually very good (especially in newbie games).
As for my mafia experience, I’ve been playing on and off in this forum for about 4 years now, except not over the past year, largely for mental health reasons. I pretty much have 2 games where I’ve even played remotely well, those are 193 and 212, read them if you want but also don’t because it’s a waste of time. I’ve also played many games on Mafia Universe (where sheep mainly comes from) where the style of play is very different.

To newbies: if you’re interested in learning how the game works, reading old games is a good idea. I recommend 142, but I’m sure scipio has better recommendations than me because he’s not retired like I am, and he knows recent games played here.

Also - good rule of thumb is don’t reveal your role. Just assume everyone is a vanilla townie. If you’re a power role we will probably get into that later, so yeah

Anyway if you have any questions just ask. Don’t forget to have fun, that’s the most important part.

I usually lurk in thread a lot of hours of the day, I’m PST and I’m not on during school usually. So mornings and evenings.

sheepsaysmeep wrote:
a general tip before I think about that exchange in terms of alignment:

it's definitely pro town, generally, to say what youre thinking and be active over staying silent, else it's effectively impossible for us to read you. even if youre inaccurate the number one thing you can do to go from being always resolved by town at some point to an easy townread is trying to practice explaining your thought process (personal experience). if everyone prefers to lurk and watch then we're basically going to be taking random potshots. also discussion is always good.

Seconding this. This is mostly why I townread Lila, and the discussion that arose from it ended up being useful. I do think it's useful to have some people that are more active and engaged, and some people that are more of listeners; this creates different social dynamics, which makes it harder for scum to cause chaos. If everyone is extremely active and throwing out a bunch of opinions, it creates an environment that's easier for wolves to manipulate.

However yes in general it's better to say things that you think of. And if you don't have any idea who's town and who's mafia, just make something up and don't throw the game in the process.

As for where I'm at overall:
Sky-Yuze feels like town, I think this
Sky-Yuze wrote:
Unvote
Vote: soggydollar just to see what he/she will say
is towny, and his thought processes are pretty clear (no contradictions/holes as of yet).

Lila, I townread her early, I definitely go back on that now. She has yet to really come into the game fully and she's really just.. not engaged with the town as a whole. But not in the same mysterious way that llr is, just in a way that reads scummy opening.

Scipio idk. GTH he's a wolf but I don't think pressure on him is useful right now. Sheep is similar but at this point is not readable/not worth reading in a newbie game. I'm trying not to be overly paranoid of him after how he tricked me in 212. So if I have to read him later I'll just use more tells than I can during early D1.

im gonna step in here from and say I dont think apw's amount of throwing around suspicion, has, in my experience, historically been wolf indicative. in a 9 player game you can say you find half the game wolfy and I could let it slide depending on the game state.

"shading people" without consequences is a kinda common wolf indicator, i.e. talking about a person as suspicious and then avoiding actually wolf reading that person, so if one day the person is hung and flips villager then the shader might not face the consequences because he technically didnt push the flipped villager. anyways personally, I dont think apw has crossed any of those boundaries, it's 100 posts into the game and the stuff he's said is fine, his reaction without losing an ounce of his cool at being pushed in this sort of fashion is fine, more of a null for me atm

also +1 to town hunting over scum hunting being a good technique, ive always been like that and actually find it intuitive, though I never think of it explicitly or prioritize it that way (of course I'd rather have found a wolf than have found a villager). it just ends up easier to find town in a strong playerlist and it's going to be more accurate than any wolfread

post_rendered:
hanging is 100% worth the risk of outing a pr.
mafia is essentially whether you can find all the mafia with a given amount of times town can "resolve" someone. hang is resolve, vig shot is resolve if used properly (this is why I think doing a hero shot outside of the poe is idiotic even if you strongly believe in yourself), cop peek etc is effectively a resolve. etc. this is why successfully protecting a vanilla town in the night is useful, because it moves you closer to getting an extra chance to resolve someone, moreso than the fact that someone gets to contribute more for a day. no offense to the saved person. anyways 2v7 at its base state is "can you find a pool of 4 that has the 2 mafia". if you no hang twice it becomes "can you find 2 mafia in a pool of 3". very bad.

Okay. We have 3 tries to get the last mafia (ignoring PRs). A poe pool of 4 town means we can eliminate the final 3 people and when the game. Not suggesting we should do this immediately but it’s just a thought for later days.

First obvious thing: I think sky-yuze is probably town. You can cry bussing or wifom or whatever but I don’t think newbie scum ever makes that hammer, and I think with the cushion we have we should absolutely not be going for sky for today.

Second: that night kill, given mossplet’s activity and the fact that they were somewhat scum read, was a pr hunt. It doesn’t necessarily rule out anyone as mafia or town but there are definitely some people in this game more likely to kill mosselt.

3rd thing - interactions and wagons. Now that we know bun was mafia, we can analyze how the rest of the game is involved, using interactions and wagons (which I think are the two most reliable ways of reading people). Obviously I was hoping to have more wagons at the end of the day so there’s not much to analyze there. Interactions tho is something I’ll try to go more in depth with later once I hop on my computer. Despite bun’s lack of posts there is always a lot to unpack after hanging a mafia.

You’re correct that the track isn’t a clear. Pretty much all NKA (night-kill analysis) is WIFOM and shouldn’t be discussed too much. It’s impossible to tell whether or not the kill was mafia not doing anything or a doctor/vp block, and thus the no movement from chriswren doesn’t mean anything useful.

In terms of yesterday’s eod and what to take forward from that:
Wagons aren’t super useful because there’s only one mafia alive. The main two wagons were me and sky, and you can try to call me scummy because of how easily the mishang happened and how it bounced off of my wagon, but even if I was scum, I would be the only scum on the sky wagon anyways, and that’s self-preservation, not rooted in a scum agenda. So it was a largely fruitless mishang. I’m sure there’s more nuanced things to analyze but with only one scum alive the scum agenda-based social dynamics don’t really show up as much.

Big picture: we have 6 people, 1 mafia, 2 conftown, 2 living power roles.

It’s hard to plan out how the power roles will play out but some things are for sure: we hang today, and if we make it into 4-person mylo then we no hang. I don’t think we’re at an advanced enough level where we have to worry about who we take into lylo so it’s best to do our best to find mafia today and if we fail then we still have a decent chance at winning.

It’s either gonna be chriswren or one of the three SEs. I have about a week left in my quarter at school so don’t expect too much analysis from me for a bit. We are getting into the point of the game where I have to actually try and read sheep and scipio. A little lost because sky was my biggest townread but I’m sure we’ll figure it out.

post_rendered:
Whoops I’ve been a little inactive.

Hypothetically in a world where we hammer on scipio, then llr (assumedly) dies and we go into mylo with two conftown, one of whom is an IC

I mean from my perspective it’s an autowin because I’m town and we hang within {chriswren, scipio1} but it’s not that simple and there’s still the possibility of me getting hanged today/tomorrow.
We’ve gone over this a million times so yeah.

So I guess that explains my level of.. unmotivation. I think in a thunder dome situation I would be more active, than in a situation where there’s several hang options and we win as long as it’s not me.

Beyond that.. going more micro.. I definitely understand scipio’s frustration. I am hang bait and always have been. Whether or not scipio is town or scum.. I feel for his position either way. I think people largely piled onto him without a great deal of deliberation, it just happened, all of a sudden. This ties back into my point of how important and valuable it is in mafia to manipulate social dynamics. If one good player, town or scum, can either create chaos (scum) or order (town) they can inexplicably change the entire direction of the game. This is getting relatively deep into mafia theory for a newbie game but hopefully the point is clear. That not always hangs happen because of overwhelming evidence, but because of town mob mentality. On the other hand, highly skilled towns usually lose because they can’t find a good hang and end up on a mishang that no one likes, due largely to chaos and mistrust sown by mafia members.


Anyway. I’m comfortable hanging scipio. If he’s scum then great, and if town is hanged today,

Then we have 3 free weeks of analysis that sheep will probably be alive for. So no rush. Sorry for the rambling, I am on mobile, just wanted to get some thoughts out (although I’m sure they are redundant as always) and I don’t wanna get replaced.

post_rendered:
llr was driving home late at night when he noticed movement in the trees. Thinking that it might be a deer, he slowed down to observe more closely. As he got closer, he realized that it was a person. Suddenly, gunfire broke out as the person in the trees began shooting at the car. llr began trying to get away, swerving the car to try to avoid the shots. As the bullets broke through the back windows of the car, the vehicle slipped off the road, careening into a nearby tree and coming to a halt. Dazed, the occupant of the vehicle began trying to get out after a few seconds. But just as he managed to extricate himself from the car, llr came face to face with his attacker. A shot rang out, and he fell to the ground dead.

The next morning, llr's body was discovered. A search of his house revealed recording equipment. It would seem that he was the Tracker

post_rendered:
well actually, I do see the idea that no hang is ineffective because it's just going to be a clear dying

just for teaching points:
lylo is like 2v1, 3v2, 4v3, etc: You have to hang, and if you hang a villager you lose. mylo is 3v1, 4v2, 5v3, etc: You can hang, and if you hang a villager you probably lose, OR you can no hang, and if hte mafia kills someone, tomorrow will be lylo. You have not lost the opportunity to hang someone when you used no hang in this case. generally what people will do is they'll use the no hang, and they'll take which person the mafia kills as information. a) they dont have to try to sort that person and b) they can think about why did the mafia not want that person in lylo? why not these ppl instead? etc. so I'll verify that what apw said is theoretically correct. in this specific situation though we really dont get much information so might as well hang today, barring the chance that we dont feel like we've had enough time to discuss at the end of 3 weeks, in which case no hang is a viable option.

It was the dead of night. A figure was crouched behind some bushes next to a house. On the front yard, the fact that the house was monitored by cameras was proudly advertised on a sign. The person observing the house was aware of the cameras and had already noted their locations earlier. Taking out their rifle, the person aimed at the first camera. A shot ran out, destroying the camera that was mounted on the wall. Neither that camera nor the other cameras could see the mysterious figure from the location they were at. The person moved again, shooting down another camera. Soon, enough had been removed such that that one could enter the house without being recorded by the cameras which were streaming to the cloud. The occupant of the home was still asleep, not having woken up since the rifle had a silencer. It was only when the door to bedroom opened that soggydollar jolted awake. The attacker shot his gun. soggydollar died. He was a Vanilla Townie.
